name: discord
description: Discord chat — exposes channels, members, message stream, and the composer for DMs and server channels.
notes: Routes by URL: discord.com/channels/@me is the DM list, /@me/<id> is a DM conversation, /<serverId>/<channelId> is a server channel. DM conversation header renders as `Discord | <DisplayName> (@<username>) (DM)` when both are available (e.g. `Discord | Jtonna (@notboosted) (DM)`), so agents can recognize that the display name and account handle refer to the same person; the response also exposes `recipientUsername` and `recipientDisplayName` as separate fields. The composer renders as `[eN] Message @<recipient> textbox` for DMs or `[eN] Message #<channel> textbox` for server channels, preserving the discriminating prefix so workflows can tell composers apart from the formatted tree alone. Boolean ARIA properties (checked/selected/expanded) are normalized from CDP's stringy 'true'/'false' values into real booleans so consumers can use === true safely. Transient UI elements — right-click context menus, the server-name dropdown in the channel list's upper-left, the Create Channel dialog, "+" buttons next to categories, and empty new categories — don't appear in the accessibility tree until interacted with. To see them, use browser_click on the affordance to summon it, then re-fetch the accessibility tree. The server-name dropdown specifically opens when clicked and contains server settings, channel creation, and role management options; it's invisible until the click summons it. Right-clicking a message exposes context actions (reactions, edit, delete, copy ID, copy link) that are not visible in the default tree — use browser_click with right-button or platform workflows to surface them. When you need raw transient UI without re-clicking to summon it, pass `usePlatformOptimizer: false` to browser_get_accessibility_tree to bypass the formatter and inspect the raw CDP tree; use this sparingly as it's noisier.

workflows.name: send_message
workflows.description: Compose a message in the active Discord channel and send it.
